mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% signals severe economic distress and slack, depressing future consumer spending and corporate earnings, which leads to negative forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE is 9.6, triggering the condition.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is positive during periods when the unemployment rate is above 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate, near the zero lower bound, acts as a powerful tailwind for equities by compressing the discount rate on future cash flows and encouraging portfolio reallocation into risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 0.19, near the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: The correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over a 5-year rolling window when FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A negative month-over-month change in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, which translates directly to lower expected corporate revenues and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The information coefficient for the 1-month change in RSAFS predicting 20-day forward returns is not statistically significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production signals that supply chains are healing and corporate activity is ramping up, leading to positive earnings surprises and equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:High UNRATE suggests a post-recessionary environment is plausible.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE > 6.5%, the correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 60-day forward returns is zero or negative.

mechanism_narrative: When monetary policy is already highly accommodative (low FEDFUNDS), a negative surprise in payrolls (PAYEMS) increases the market's expectation of further central bank support, causing a positive short-term equity market reaction.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, the correlation between the 1-month change in PAYEMS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts, defined as a year-over-year decline greater than 20%, is a strong leading indicator of a broad-based recession and bear market for equities.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger of the HOUST decline signal.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) while unemployment (UNRATE) is already high creates a stagflationary environment, constraining the central bank's ability to ease and negatively impacting equity valuations via higher discount rates and lower margins.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate for predicting negative 20-day returns is below 55% over a walk-forward test covering at least one full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strengthening labor market, which boosts aggregate income and consumer spending, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive over any 10-year rolling window.
